title: Classical Music You Would Have Liked to See
display_title: Classical Music You Would Have Liked to See
wikitext:
[]: Classical Music You Would Have Liked to See?             Quote Post by beaverteeth92 » Sun Oct 03, 2010 11:52 pm What classical music that wasn't used on the show would you liked to have heard at least once? I always thought Liszt's Liebestraum No. 3 or Debussy's Clair de Lune would have been amazing for some of the more emotional scenes, with basically anything by Wagner for fight scenes. Samuel Barber's Adagio for Strings and the Elgar Cello Concerto could also have been good. Actually, Shinji could have been shown playing that concerto. If it was the Du Pre version, I doubt too many people could watch the series without weeping.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Mon Oct 04, 2010 12:05 am Anything by Wagner. [/The_Imperialist]

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Mon Oct 04, 2010 12:16 am That one piece by Satie that Anno uses in... one of his LA films (I forget which).

[]:             Quote Post by Oz » Mon Oct 04, 2010 6:36 am Eva Yojimbo wrote: That one piece by Satie that Anno uses in... one of his LA films (I forget which).

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Mon Oct 04, 2010 7:04 am Eva Yojimbo wrote: That one piece by Satie that Anno uses in... one of his LA films (I forget which).

[]:             Quote Post by Oz » Mon Oct 04, 2010 7:14 am Xard wrote: Propably Love & Pop. Albeit AFAIK it wasn't Gymnopedie

[]:             Quote Post by The-Happy-Go-Lucky-Wacko » Mon Oct 04, 2010 8:39 am Seeing Shostakovich's Waltz Number 2 would be pretty interesting.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Mon Oct 04, 2010 1:18 pm I always think Brahms' Nänie should have been in there somewhere. If not for the fact that the entire Ode to Joy sequence is the single best in the show, I'd suggest that it go there. Or maybe somewhere during EoE? Like GNR disintegrating...the first half of Expansion of Blockade doesn't really do anything for me (nothing until the trombones show up).

[]:             Quote Post by InstrumentalityOne » Mon Oct 04, 2010 3:06 pm It´s not really classical music but snippets of Keith Jarret´s The Köln Concert would´ve been awesome in some parts.

[]:             Quote Post by Uriel Septim VII » Mon Oct 04, 2010 6:10 pm I would like to have seen Borodin's Polovtsian Dances for the opening credits as was originally intended. And Beethoven's 7th, 2nd Movement.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Mon Oct 04, 2010 9:54 pm Oz wrote: ... that would have guaranteed EoE the "Official Seal of Artsy Pretension" on its own. Gymnopedie is in every freakin' DEEP arthouse film - at least in Japan.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Mon Oct 04, 2010 10:01 pm Aww, I was hoping this was going to be about synesthesia.

[]:             Quote Post by cyharding » Mon Oct 04, 2010 11:02 pm I would have liked to hear pieces from Holst's The Planets. Primarily "Uranus, the Magician" for Kawrou and "Neptune, the Mystic" for the conclusion of EoE.

[]:             Quote Post by Uriel Septim VII » Mon Oct 04, 2010 11:08 pm And "Saturn, the Bringer of Old Age" for Instrumentality.

[]:             Quote Post by DevRei17 » Tue Oct 05, 2010 6:31 am I don't know the name of it but that piece they used in X2: X-Men United (When Nightcrawler attacks the White House.) I could hear that being used Kaworu's descent on Nerv in episode 24.

[]:             Quote Post by berto » Wed Oct 06, 2010 5:54 pm Mozart's requiem, dies inrae. personally i'd go for chopin. probably the mid part, and only the mid part, of the funeral march. http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=kyFyAqLtHq8 starts at around 2:25

[]:             Quote Post by chee » Thu Oct 07, 2010 1:25 am Wozzeck.

[]:             Quote Post by ran1 » Thu Oct 07, 2010 6:29 am Kazan's New World Symphony always struck me as an interesting possibility, especially in EoE Any part of Mozart's Requiem would have been cool as well.

[]:             Quote Post by chaosakita » Fri Oct 08, 2010 1:05 am I think the Marriage of Figaro would be a good fit. Or La donna è mobile, if thematically.

[]:             Quote Post by berto » Fri Oct 08, 2010 1:28 pm I would say Beethoven's Choral Fantasy but it sounds too much like the Ode to Joy, it would just be redundant.

[]: Classical Music You Would Have Liked to See?             Quote Post by beaverteeth92 » Sun Oct 03, 2010 11:52 pm What classical music that wasn't used on the show would you liked to have heard at least once? I always thought Liszt's Liebestraum No. 3 or Debussy's Clair de Lune would have been amazing for some of the more emotional scenes, with basically anything by Wagner for fight scenes. Samuel Barber's Adagio for Strings and the Elgar Cello Concerto could also have been good. Actually, Shinji could have been shown playing that concerto. If it was the Du Pre version, I doubt too many people could watch the series without weeping.
